severity_rationale: Permanent per-account correlation identifier (auth_token_account_id) not rotated — same value today as 2 days ago in v001. Exposed in URL on every store->api SSO round-trip. v001 flagged the leak, but the NEW finding here is confirmed NON-ROTATION across remediation window. Reportable as 'post-remediation: identifier not rotated'.
validation_method: Curl — captured redirect URL query at hop 2; compared against v001 artifact (redirect-chain-evidence.txt).
response_summary: auth_token_account_id=rcVobEe+JpAshojPvHU9ArXf//8fzXNnCsERjQnZjZY= — IDENTICAL value to report-v001 captured 2026-04-16. No rotation despite remediation of the redirect sink.
impact: Account-identifier-in-URL anti-pattern. Leaked via browser history, HTTP logs, CDN logs, Referer on any resource loaded during hop 2. Value is opaque (base64) but STABLE — single leak enables permanent tracking of victim across all future OAuth flows from store.
chain: Enables F-A-001 / F-A-003 to yield a stable correlator.
remediation: (1) Rotate auth_token_account_id; (2) do not pass in URL — use server-side session or encrypted cookie; (3) if rotation is expensive, at minimum remove from query string on the store->api hop.

reportability_audit.Q3_impact_demonstrated: PASS — same value across sessions, documented
reportability_audit.prior_report_similarity: 0.55 — PARTIAL: v001 flagged the leak; the NEW element is proof of non-rotation. Report as follow-up info-disclosure.
